title: Optimisations in OPT_LUT pass
body:
Using the command line

`yosys -p synth_ice40 relut.v`

this code:

```
module top (input clk,output reg[3:0]z,input[3:0]x,input[3:0]y,input s);
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    z<=x+(s?y:0); // 7 LUTs
  end
endmodule
```

is synthesised differently from this code:

```
module top (input clk,output reg[3:0]z,input[3:0]x,input[3:0]y,input s);
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    z<=s?(x+y):x; // 4 LUTs
  end
endmodule
```

the second style being much more efficient as the OPT_LUT pass succeeds in packing all the logic into one LC per bit.

One result of this is that multipliers synthesised from LUTs, which as far as I can tell are essentially constructed using the first style, are much bigger than they need to be.

I am using Yosys 0.9+932 (git sha1 65f197e2, clang 6.0.0-1ubuntu2 -fPIC -Os).
code_blocks:
module top (input clk,output reg[3:0]z,input[3:0]x,input[3:0]y,input s);
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    z<=x+(s?y:0); // 7 LUTs
  end
endmodule

module top (input clk,output reg[3:0]z,input[3:0]x,input[3:0]y,input s);
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    z<=s?(x+y):x; // 4 LUTs
  end
endmodule
